# setObject(_:atIndexedSubscript:)

**Instance Method**

Sets the value’s JavaScript property at the specified index, allowing subscript syntax.

## Declaration

```swift
func setObject(_ object: Any!, atIndexedSubscript index: Int)
```

## Parameters

- **object**: The value to set at the specified index.
- **index**: An index in the JavaScript object.

## Discussion

This method is equivalent to the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/setValue(_:at:)] method, but provides Objective-C subscripting support.

## Accessing Values with Subscript Syntax

- **objectAtIndexedSubscript(_:)**: Returns the value’s JavaScript property at the specified index, allowing subscript syntax.
- **objectForKeyedSubscript(_:)**: Returns the value’s JavaScript property named with the specified key, allowing subscript syntax.
- **setObject(_:forKeyedSubscript:)**: Sets the value’s JavaScript property named with the specified key, allowing subscript syntax.
